description: CLI interface for Brickfreedom dashboard automation

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.
login.usage_instructions: Use `auth login` for configure authentication credentials. prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. for oauth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent..

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

logout.help: Clear stored credentials and browser sessions.
logout.usage_instructions: Use `auth logout` for clear stored credentials and browser sessions..

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

profiles.usage_instructions: Use `auth profiles` for manage authentication profiles.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Use `auth profiles create` for create a new profile from .env.example template..

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Use `auth profiles delete` for delete a profile and its data..

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.
get.usage_instructions: Use `auth profiles get` for get details for a specific profile..

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.
list.usage_instructions: Use `auth profiles list` for list all profiles and show which is the default..

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

remove.help: Delete a profile and its data.

select.help: Activate a profile within its auth type.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to activate within its auth type

status.usage_instructions: Use `auth status` for check authentication status across profiles..

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Use `auth test` for test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles..

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Use `cache clear` for remove all cached responses. deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed..

get.help: Get a specific order by marketplace order ID. Example: brickfreedom order get 30070421
get.usage_instructions: Use `order get` for get a specific order by marketplace order id. example: brickfreedom order get 30070421.

list.help: List orders from BrickFreedom orders page. Example: brickfreedom order list brickfreedom order list --status PAID brickfreedom order list --platform bricklink --table brickfreedom order list --picked true brickfreedom order list --filter status:eq:PAID
list.usage_instructions: Use `order list` for list orders from brickfreedom orders page. example: brickfreedom order list brickfreedom order list --status paid brickfreedom order list --platform bricklink --table brickfreedom order list --picked true brickfreedom order list --filter status:eq:paid.

options.name: --status
options.help: Filter by status (PAID, PACKED, SHIPPED, etc.)

post.help: Post one or more orders (mark as shipped on marketplace). All orders must have tracking numbers before posting. Example: brickfreedom order post 30070421 brickfreedom order post 30070421 30070422
post.usage_instructions: Use `order post` for post one or more orders (mark as shipped on marketplace). all orders must have tracking numbers before posting. example: brickfreedom order post 30070421 brickfreedom order post 30070421 30070422.

arguments.name: order_ids
arguments.help: ORDER_IDS...  Marketplace order IDs to post (mark as shipped)

process.help: Mark one or more orders as processed by marketplace order ID. Example: brickfreedom order process 30070421 brickfreedom order process 30070421 30070422 30070423
process.usage_instructions: Use `order process` for mark one or more orders as processed by marketplace order id. example: brickfreedom order process 30070421 brickfreedom order process 30070421 30070422 30070423.

arguments.name: order_ids
arguments.help: ORDER_IDS...  Marketplace order IDs to mark as processed

processed.help: List processed orders ready for shipping (from order-postage page). Example: brickfreedom order processed brickfreedom order processed --table
processed.usage_instructions: Use `order processed` for list processed orders ready for shipping (from order-postage page). example: brickfreedom order processed brickfreedom order processed --table.

tracking.help: Set tracking number for an order on order-postage page. Example: brickfreedom order tracking 30070421 9400111899223847012345
tracking.usage_instructions: Use `order tracking` for set tracking number for an order on order-postage page. example: brickfreedom order tracking 30070421 9400111899223847012345.

complete.help: Mark a task as complete by 1-based index, by content match, or --bulk. Three modes: 1. Positional index (interactive use, fragile for scripts): brickfreedom task complete 1 2. Bulk: brickfreedom task complete --bulk 3. Match by missing-part attributes (stable for scripts — re-resolves the current index right before completing, so prior list-shifting completions cannot mark the wrong task): brickfreedom task complete \ --match-platform bricklink \ --match-order-id 30823995 \ --match-item-number 75270-1 Add --match-quantity to disambiguate if multiple rows match. Exit codes for match mode: - 0: exactly one match, completed. - 1: zero matches OR multiple matches (ambiguous). JSON error printed to stdout describing the failure; resolve by passing more --match-* flags.
complete.usage_instructions: Use `task complete` for mark a task as complete by 1-based index, by content match, or --bulk. three modes: 1. positional index (interactive use, fragile for scripts): brickfreedom task complete 1 2. bulk: brickfreedom task complete --bulk 3. match by missing-part attributes (stable for scripts — re-resolves the current index right before completing, so prior list-shifting completions cannot mark the wrong task): brickfreedom task complete \ --match-platform bricklink \ --match-order-id 30823995 \ --match-item-number 75270-1 add --match-quantity to disambiguate if multiple rows match. exit codes for match mode: - 0: exactly one match, completed. - 1: zero matches or multiple matches (ambiguous). json error printed to stdout describing the failure; resolve by passing more --match-* flags..

options.name: --match-platform
options.help: Match a missing-part task by platform (bricklink|brickowl). Re-resolves the current task index by content. Cannot be combined with positional index

options.name: --match-order-id
options.help: Match a missing-part task by marketplace order ID. Cannot be combined with positional index

options.name: --match-item-number
options.help: Match a missing-part task by LEGO item number. Cannot be combined with positional index

options.name: --match-quantity
options.help: Match a missing-part task by quantity (use to disambiguate when multiple rows match). Cannot be combined with positional index

create.help: Create a new task in My Tasks. Example: brickfreedom task create "Ship pending orders" brickfreedom task add "Reply to customer" # Create structured replacement part task: brickfreedom task create --type customer-replacement-part \ --platform bricklink \ --customer-name "John Doe" \ --order-id "30176576" \ --item-no "3024" \ --item-name "Plate 1 x 1" \ --color "Light Bluish Gray" \ --qty 10 \ --location "I-BB001" # Create structured missing part task: brickfreedom task create --type missing-part \ --platform bricklink \ --order-id "30823995" \ --item-no "75270-1" \ --item-name "Instruction Book" \ --qty 1 \ --location "F-INS" # Missing part with color: brickfreedom task create --type missing-part \ --platform bricklink \ --order-id "30823995" \ --item-no "3008" \ --item-name "Brick 1 x 8" \ --color "Light Aqua" \ --qty 2 \ --location "C-0961"
create.usage_instructions: Use `task create` for create a new task in my tasks. example: brickfreedom task create "ship pending orders" brickfreedom task add "reply to customer" # create structured replacement part task: brickfreedom task create --type customer-replacement-part \ --platform bricklink \ --customer-name "john doe" \ --order-id "30176576" \ --item-no "3024" \ --item-name "plate 1 x 1" \ --color "light bluish gray" \ --qty 10 \ --location "i-bb001" # create structured missing part task: brickfreedom task create --type missing-part \ --platform bricklink \ --order-id "30823995" \ --item-no "75270-1" \ --item-name "instruction book" \ --qty 1 \ --location "f-ins" # missing part with color: brickfreedom task create --type missing-part \ --platform bricklink \ --order-id "30823995" \ --item-no "3008" \ --item-name "brick 1 x 8" \ --color "light aqua" \ --qty 2 \ --location "c-0961".

arguments.name: text
arguments.help: Task text to create (not needed for --type)

options.name: --platform
options.help: Platform: bricklink or brickowl (required for customer-replacement-part)

delete.help: Delete a completed task by index, or all completed tasks with --completed. Task must be completed before it can be deleted. Example: brickfreedom task delete 1 brickfreedom task delete --completed
delete.usage_instructions: Use `task delete` for delete a completed task by index, or all completed tasks with --completed. task must be completed before it can be deleted. example: brickfreedom task delete 1 brickfreedom task delete --completed.

get.help: Get a specific task by index. Example: brickfreedom task get 1 brickfreedom task get 2 --table
get.usage_instructions: Use `task get` for get a specific task by index. example: brickfreedom task get 1 brickfreedom task get 2 --table.

list.help: List tasks from My Tasks section on dashboard. Example: brickfreedom task list brickfreedom task list --table brickfreedom task list --filter completed:eq:false brickfreedom task list --type customer-replacement-part brickfreedom task list --type customer-replacement-part --order-id 30176576
list.usage_instructions: Use `task list` for list tasks from my tasks section on dashboard. example: brickfreedom task list brickfreedom task list --table brickfreedom task list --filter completed:eq:false brickfreedom task list --type customer-replacement-part brickfreedom task list --type customer-replacement-part --order-id 30176576.

options.name: --type
options.help: [customer-replacement-part|missing-part]  Filter by task type: customer-replacement-part, missing-part

options.name: --debug-unparsed
options.help: Print raw task rows that did not match any known missing-part format (to stderr, prefixed with ' ')

usage_instructions: Use `brickfreedom` for cli interface for brickfreedom dashboard automation.
